introSection:
Flip Flap Railway was a wooden roller coaster located at Paul Boyton's Sea Lion Park in Brooklyn, New York, USA.

Flip Flap Railway was built at Sea Lion Park in 1895. The ride was developed by Lina Beecher and had a single vertical loop. At in diameter, the loop was too small and too circular and thus subjected riders to dangerous levels of g-force. It reportedly snapped riders' necks. The ride had been dismantled by 1903, when the park was redeveloped into Luna Park.

The ride was the first looping coaster since the 1840s and it sparked a renewed interest in looping coasters.
fullText:
Flip Flap Railway was a wooden roller coaster located at Paul Boyton's Sea Lion Park in Brooklyn, New York, USA.

Flip Flap Railway was built at Sea Lion Park in 1895. The ride was developed by Lina Beecher and had a single vertical loop. At in diameter, the loop was too small and too circular and thus subjected riders to dangerous levels of g-force. It reportedly snapped riders' necks. The ride had been dismantled by 1903, when the park was redeveloped into Luna Park.

The ride was the first looping coaster since the 1840s and it sparked a renewed interest in looping coasters.
